para_trans: Assistant Assistant Commissioner of Charitable Endowments, Commissioner, Yakt. Periodic transfer of Group-A Group-A Group-A officers - 2026
Government of Maharashtra
Department of Law and Justice
GOVERNMENT ORDER NO. - CHA-2026 / Q.No. 01 / ACTION-1 (E-1663760)
Madam Cama Marg, Martyrdom Rajguru Chowk,
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400 032.
Date: 26th May 2026.
the Government Order

In accordance with the provisions of Section 4 (3) of the Maharashtra Prevention of Delay in Performing Government Duties and Regulation of Transfer of Government Employees Act, 2005 and with the approval of the competent authorities, the Assistant Commissioner of Charities, Group-A Cadre, on the establishment of a charitable organisation under the administrative control of the Department of Law and Justice, is appointing the officers mentioned in the table below to the post indicated in column (4) before their NAB with periodic transfer.
table_trans: Assistant Commissioner of Charitable Endowments, Ayukt, Buldhana

para_trans: 2. The Charity Commissioner, State of Maharashtra shall immediately relieve the officers concerned from their present position by making such alternate local arrangements as may be necessary so that the transferred officers may take charge immediately at the place of their transfer and shall forthwith at the place of their transfer.
Instruct them to join.
3. Maharashtra Civil Service (Retirement period, Non-Service and Suspension, Dismissal and demotion from service)

The officer concerned shall take care of joining the place of the new posting within the prescribed period as per the rules in the Provisions of the Removal) Rules, 1981. Otherwise, the period of their absence is a "non-working day" (dies) if they do not join the office of this office.
will be counted as non). Also, the concerned should note that disciplinary action will be taken against the concerned.
d. the period of assumption of office, travel allowance etc., as may be admissible to the officers concerned in accordance with the rules.
Contingent allowances will remain payable.

No leave of any kind shall be granted to the officers concerned without the prior approval of the Government and no leave applications shall be entertained of officers posted by the original office in accordance with Rule 30 (c) of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1981.

6. In case the officer concerned does not immediately join the post of posting as per the said order or attempts to cancel and alter the transfer by any kind of direct or indirect pressure, disciplinary action will be taken against them as it is misconduct as the act violates Rule 23 of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1979.
7. Written report after the officer concerned appears for transfer.
The charity should be submitted to the government through the commissioner's office.
8. This Government Order is available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra www.maharashtra.gov.in.
